response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong technical depth across 16+ years of software development and has some exposure to infrastructure and CI/CD work, this role explicitly requires 2 years of people management experience—which is entirely absent from the resume. Additionally, developer tools building experience is limited (2 years of infrastructure/CI work vs. 3 years required), with no evidence of leading organizational change. This is a full-time people-leadership role, and the candidate's background is primarily that of a senior individual contributor, not a manager.
strengths: 16+ years of software development experience across diverse domains (backend, frontend, mobile, embedded systems, infrastructure)
Strong teaching and mentorship track record through workshops, courses, and open-source contributions
Demonstrated technical leadership through senior and staff roles with hands-on expertise in system design and architecture
concerns: No evidence of people management experience—conducts no one-on-ones, performance reviews, or team development (critical gap for Level II Manager role)

Developer tools experience limited to ~2 years of CI/CD/infrastructure work (GitLab, deploy servers, Docker) versus required 3 years of building developer tools (compilers, test automation, code design tools)
No evidence of leading organizational change initiatives or advanced degree; mobile platform experience is 10+ years outdated

key_factors.factor: People management experience (0 years vs. 2 required)
Developer tools building focus (2 years infrastructure vs. 3 required)
Technical depth and software development breadth (16+ years)
